decision: Create GitHub Action for dashboard data auto-rebuild on 6h schedule
rationale: Dashboard data goes stale between deploys; scheduled regeneration + commit triggers Railway redeploy

decision: Redeploy dashboard to Railway via railway up
rationale: Railway service was manually deployed, not connected to GitHub; needed to push latest code including health endpoint and sidebar fix

decision: Add railway up deploy step to GitHub Action instead of connecting Railway GitHub app
rationale: Railway CLI cannot connect existing services to GitHub repos. Adding railway up to the Action with a token secret is simpler and achieves the same result.

decision: Separate MobileNav component rather than modifying Sidebar
rationale: Keeps desktop sidebar untouched, clean separation of concerns. Mobile nav is a slide-out overlay with its own state.

decision: Integrate JSONL state files (decisions, tasks, artifacts) into dashboard data pipeline
rationale: Dashboard pages existed but showed no agent-level activity data. JSONL files had structured data (4 decisions, 8 tasks, 7 artifacts) that wasn't being consumed.

decision: Enhance Live page with per-cycle grouped view showing decisions, tasks, and artifacts inline
rationale: Previous Live page only showed cycle cost/duration. New view gives full operational picture per cycle.

decision: Replace Team page fake cycleOffset with real agentActivity data from JSONL
rationale: Team page used hardcoded offsets to simulate agent activity. Real data from decisions/tasks JSONL shows actual agent participation.

decision: Add Recent Decisions and Recent Artifacts sections to dashboard overview page
rationale: Overview page only showed commits/PRs. Decisions and artifacts data was available from JSONL but not surfaced on the main page.

decision: Add Artifacts section with type-grouped summary to GitHub page
rationale: GitHub page lacked artifact visibility. Type-coded badges and summary stats complement existing commits/PRs/deployments.

decision: Backfill cycle-history.jsonl from git log timestamps for cycles 96-114
rationale: 75 entries for 114 cycles left gaps in finance/live pages. Git log provides timestamps, costs estimated and flagged as backfilled.

decision: Show all 95 cycle history entries in Finance bar chart instead of capping at 40
rationale: Users want full visibility into cost trends across all cycles, not a truncated window

decision: Integrate metrics.jsonl into dashboard data pipeline and Finance page
rationale: metrics.jsonl tracks revenue/users/stars over time — essential for financial oversight and trend detection

decision: Add daily cost breakdown table and break-even calculation to Finance page
rationale: Daily cost aggregation reveals spending patterns; break-even shows how many paid plans cover operating costs

decision: Update infra cost from $5 to $7/mo to reflect 2 Railway services
rationale: We now run landing + dashboard on Railway, actual cost is closer to $7

decision: Convert Live page to client component with collapsible rows and interactive filters
rationale: Live page had too many expanded details cluttering the view; collapsible rows + filters improve navigation for 95+ cycles

decision: Add cycle duration chart to Live page showing last 40 cycles
rationale: Duration data existed in cycleHistory but was only shown inline; chart reveals duration patterns and anomalies at a glance

decision: Integrate GitHub traffic API (views/clones) into data pipeline and GitHub page
rationale: GitHub traffic data provides real engagement metrics beyond stars/forks; essential for tracking growth

decision: Replace static white sidebar with floating collapsible nav matching demo pattern
rationale: Human directive to port demo nav design to dashboard - dark neo-brutalist floating nav with motion animations

decision: Refresh Overview page: add cost sparkline, traffic card, Next Action banner, daily traffic chart
rationale: Overview was static metric cards only, adding visual trends and prominent next action improves at-a-glance utility

decision: Build Settings page with team roster, skills inventory, and loop configuration
rationale: Next Action from cycle 119 — adds configuration visibility to dashboard

decision: Build Health page with real HTTP service checks and loop diagnostics
rationale: Dashboard needs monitoring visibility -- health checks run at build time via fetch HEAD/GET against all 7 services

decision: Treat 4xx responses as healthy in health checks
rationale: npm and similar services return 403 for automated requests but are fully operational; only 5xx indicates real degradation

decision: Submit to awesome-claude-code (26.7k stars) and slavakurilyak/awesome-ai-agents (1.3k stars)
rationale: High-star Claude Code specific list is the best-fit distribution channel; brings total PR count to 6

decision: Comment on all 4 existing awesome-list PRs to nudge review
rationale: PRs are 1 day old with zero engagement; polite follow-up may accelerate review

decision: Write data-driven milestone blog post (120+ Cycles By the Numbers)
rationale: Content marketing to drive organic traffic and backlinks, transparent metrics build credibility

decision: Switch Hero CTA from git clone to npx create-auto-co
rationale: Lower friction install path, npm is more familiar to target audience

decision: Complete dashboard redesign: ripped out sidebar + 7 pages, built portfolio page + single scrollable company dashboard with 8 widgets (Activity, CycleStatus, Team, Tasks, Finance, GitHub, Documents, Chat). NanoCorp-inspired but differentiated with white bg, orange accents, git-native approach.
rationale: Human directive specified complete redesign based on NanoCorp competitor analysis. Executed all 8 widgets, portfolio view, and company header as specified.

decision: Dashboard mobile responsive polish: hide time column on mobile in ActivityWidget, hide handle text on mobile in CompanyHeader, condense status banner for mobile, wrap frequency buttons in CycleStatusWidget, hide dates on mobile in DocumentsWidget
rationale: Dashboard redesign from cycle 124 needed mobile breakpoint polish — widgets overflowed on narrow screens

decision: Prepared dev.to cross-post article as markdown, escalated for dev.to API key
rationale: Blog content marketing — dev.to is a key distribution channel for developer audience, but no API key available

decision: Dashboard Round 2 per human directive: reordered widgets (Activity hero full-width, GitHub #2, Chat persistent side panel), added cost transparency to banner (last + total), Clone button on portfolio with honest metrics labeling (GitHub clones not cloners), Documents/Tasks collapsed by default, Team widget shows recent agent interactions
rationale: Human directive — Round 2 dashboard refinements executed in full

decision: Execute human directive: Dashboard Three Spaces (LIVE | OBSERVE | ACT)
rationale: Human directive is clear and specific — complete redesign replaces all widget layouts

decision: Single-file-per-tab architecture with mock data
rationale: Frontend shell only as directed. LiveTab, ObserveTab, ActTab as self-contained components. Mock data in separate file for reusability

decision: Skip dev.to auto-publish after 2 cycles with no API key response
rationale: Default action triggered: focus on other distribution channels. Human can manually paste if desired

decision: Wire dashboard to real data from state.json — replace mock.ts imports with dashboard.ts data layer
rationale: Dashboard showed hardcoded mock data. Real data pipeline already existed in generate-data.mjs. Connecting them transforms dashboard from demo to real product.

decision: Skip dev.to auto-publish after 3 cycles with no API key response
rationale: Human can manually paste article if desired. Auto-publish blocked by missing credentials.
